member.name: T:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.Internal.PromptExecutionSettingsExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods for to support response format operations.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.Internal.PromptExecutionSettingsExtensions.SupportsResponseFormat(Microsoft.SemanticKernel.PromptExecutionSettings)
member.summary: Determines whether the object supports a "ResponseFormat" property of type .
member.param: The instance to check.
member.returns: true if the "ResponseFormat" property exists and is of type ; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.Internal.PromptExecutionSettingsExtensions.SetResponseFormat``1(Microsoft.SemanticKernel.PromptExecutionSettings)
member.summary: Sets the "ResponseFormat" property of the object to the specified response type.
member.typeparam: The type to set as the response format.
member.param: The instance to update.

member.name: T:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticOrchestrationLogMessages
member.summary: Extensions for logging .
member.remarks: This extension uses the to generate logging code at compile time to achieve optimized code.

member.name: T:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManager
member.summary: A manager that manages the flow of a Magentic style chat.

member.name: F:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManager.DefaultMaximumResetsCount
member.summary: The default maximum number of resets allowed.

member.name: F:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManager.DefaultMaximumStallCount
member.summary: The default maximum number of stalls allowed.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManager.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManager.MaximumInvocationCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of invocations allowed for the group chat manager.

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManager.MaximumResetCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of resets allowed for the group chat manager.

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManager.MaximumStallCount
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of stalls allowed for the group chat manager.

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManager.InteractiveCallback
member.summary: Gets or sets the callback to be invoked for interactive input.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManager.PlanAsync(Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManagerContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Prepares the chat messages for the next step in the group chat process.
member.param: The context for the manager.
A cancellation token.
member.returns: An array of chat message content to be processed.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManager.ReplanAsync(Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManagerContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Resets the group chat state and prepares the initial chat messages.
member.param: The context for the manager.
A cancellation token.
member.returns: An array of chat message content representing the reset state.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManager.EvaluateTaskProgressAsync(Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManagerContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Evaluates the progress of the current group chat task.
member.param: The context for the manager.
A cancellation token.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManager.PrepareFinalAnswerAsync(Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManagerContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Prepares the final answer for the group chat task.
member.param: The context for the manager.
A cancellation token.
member.remarks: The return type is to allow for rich content responses.

member.name: T:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManagerActor
member.summary: An used to manage a .

member.name: F:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManagerActor.DefaultDescription
member.summary: A common description for the manager.
Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The unique identifier of the agent.
The runtime associated with the agent.
The orchestration context.
The manages the flow of the group-chat.
The team of agents being orchestrated
Identifies the orchestration agent.
The logger to use for the actor

member.name: T:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManagerContext
member.summary: Represents the context for the MagenticManager, encapsulating the team, task, and chat history.
Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The team associated with the context.
The current task or objective for the team.
The chat message history relevant to the current context (not agent conversation history).
The number of responses generated in the current context.
The number of times the context has stalled or not progressed.
The number of times the context has been reset.

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManagerContext.History
member.summary: Gets the chat message history for the current context.
member.remarks: This history refers to the overall context history, not the conversation history of a specific agent.

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManagerContext.ResponseCount
member.summary: The number of responses generated in the current context.

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManagerContext.StallCount
member.summary: The number of times the context has stalled or not progressed.

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManagerContext.ResetCount
member.summary: The number of times the context has been reset.

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManagerContext.Team
member.summary: Gets the team associated with this context.

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Magentic.MagenticManagerContext.Task
member.summary: Gets the current task or objective for the team, as provided as orchestration input.

member.summary: Common messages used for agent chat patterns.

member.summary: An empty message instance as a default.

member.summary: Extension methods for .

member.summary: Provides a name for the agent, even if it's the identifier. (since allows null)
member.param: The target agent
member.returns: The agent name as a non-empty string

member.summary: Provides the display name of the agent.
member.param: The target agent
member.remarks: Currently, it's intended for telemetry purposes only.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Extensions.AgentExtensions.GetKernel(Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.Agent,Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Agents.AgentInvokeOptions)
member.summary: Gets the kernel scoped to the current invocation.
member.param: The whose kernel is used as a fallback if does not specify one.
The instance containing invocation-specific options. May be null.
member.returns: The instance to use for the current invocation. Returns the kernel from if specified; otherwise, returns the kernel from .

member.summary: Starts an activity with the specified name and tags.

member.summary: Adds tags to the activity.

member.summary: Adds an event to the activity. Should only be used for events that contain sensitive data.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Diagnostics.ActivityExtensions.SetError(System.Diagnostics.Activity,System.Exception)
member.summary: Sets the error status and type on the activity.

member.name: T:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Diagnostics.ModelDiagnostics
member.summary: Model diagnostics helper class that provides a set of methods to trace model activities with the OTel semantic conventions. This class contains experimental features and may change in the future. To enable these features, set one of the following switches to true: `Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Experimental.GenAI.EnableOTelDiagnostics` `Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Experimental.GenAI.EnableOTelDiagnosticsSensitive` Or set the following environment variables to true: `SEMANTICKERNEL_EXPERIMENTAL_GENAI_ENABLE_OTEL_DIAGNOSTICS` `SEMANTICKERNEL_EXPERIMENTAL_GENAI_ENABLE_OTEL_DIAGNOSTICS_SENSITIVE`

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Diagnostics.ModelDiagnostics.StartCompletionActivity``1(System.Uri,System.String,System.String,System.String,``0)
member.summary: Start a text completion activity for a given model. The activity will be tagged with the a set of attributes specified by the semantic conventions.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Diagnostics.ModelDiagnostics.StartCompletionActivity``1(System.Uri,System.String,System.String,Microsoft.SemanticKernel.ChatCompletion.ChatHistory,``0)
member.summary: Start a chat completion activity for a given model. The activity will be tagged with the a set of attributes specified by the semantic conventions.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Diagnostics.ModelDiagnostics.StartAgentInvocationActivity(System.String,System.String,System.String,Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Kernel,System.Collections.Generic.ICollection{Microsoft.SemanticKernel.ChatMessageContent})
member.summary: Start an agent invocation activity and return the activity.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Diagnostics.ModelDiagnostics.SetAgentResponse(System.Diagnostics.Activity,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.SemanticKernel.ChatMessageContent})
member.summary: Set the agent response for a given activity.

member.summary: End the agent streaming response for a given activity.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Diagnostics.ModelDiagnostics.SetCompletionResponse(System.Diagnostics.Activity,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.SemanticKernel.TextContent},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Set the text completion response for a given activity. The activity will be enriched with the response attributes specified by the semantic conventions.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Diagnostics.ModelDiagnostics.SetCompletionResponse(System.Diagnostics.Activity,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.SemanticKernel.ChatMessageContent},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Set the chat completion response for a given activity. The activity will be enriched with the response attributes specified by the semantic conventions.
Notify the end of streaming for a given activity.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Diagnostics.ModelDiagnostics.SetResponseId(System.Diagnostics.Activity,System.String)
member.summary: Set the response id for a given activity.
member.param: The activity to set the response id
The response id
member.returns: The activity with the response id set for chaining

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Diagnostics.ModelDiagnostics.SetInputTokensUsage(System.Diagnostics.Activity,System.Int32)
member.summary: Set the input tokens usage for a given activity.
member.param: The activity to set the input tokens usage
The number of input tokens used
member.returns: The activity with the input tokens usage set for chaining

member.summary: Set the output tokens usage for a given activity.
member.param: The activity to set the output tokens usage
The number of output tokens used
member.returns: The activity with the output tokens usage set for chaining

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Diagnostics.ModelDiagnostics.IsModelDiagnosticsEnabled
member.summary: Check if model diagnostics is enabled Model diagnostics is enabled if either EnableModelDiagnostics or EnableSensitiveEvents is set to true and there are listeners.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Diagnostics.ModelDiagnostics.IsSensitiveEventsEnabled
member.summary: Check if sensitive events are enabled. Sensitive events are enabled if EnableSensitiveEvents is set to true and there are listeners.

member.summary: Convert a chat model response to a JSON string based on the OTel GenAI Semantic Conventions format

member.summary: Set the completion response for a given activity. The `formatCompletions` delegate won't be invoked if events are disabled.

member.name: T:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Throw
member.summary: Defines static methods used to throw exceptions.
member.remarks: The main purpose is to reduce code size, improve performance, and standardize exception messages.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Throw.IfNull``1(``0,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified argument is .
member.typeparam: Argument type to be checked for .
member.param: Object to be checked for .
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Throw.IfNullOrMemberNull``2(``0,``1,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified argument is , or if the specified member is .
member.typeparam: Argument type to be checked for .
Member type to be checked for .
member.param: Argument to be checked for .
Object member to be checked for .
The name of the parameter being checked.
The name of the member.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Throw.IfMemberNull``2(``0,``1,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified member is .
member.typeparam: Member type to be checked for .
member.param: Argument to which member belongs.
Object member to be checked for .
The name of the parameter being checked.
The name of the member.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Throw.IfNullOrWhitespace(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws either an or an if the specified string is or whitespace respectively.
member.param: String to be checked for or whitespace.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Throw.IfNullOrEmpty(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the string is , or if it is empty.
member.param: String to be checked for or empty.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Throw.IfBufferTooSmall(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the argument's buffer size is less than the required buffer size.
member.param: The actual buffer size.
The required buffer size.
The name of the parameter to be checked.

member.summary: Throws an if the enum value is not valid.
member.param: The argument to evaluate.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.summary: Throws an if the collection is , or if it is empty.
member.param: The collection to evaluate.
The name of the parameter being checked.
member.typeparam: The type of objects in the collection.

member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.

member.summary: Throws an .

A message that describes the error.

member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Throw.ArgumentException(System.String,System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.
The exception that is the cause of the current exception.
member.remarks: If the is not a , the current exception is raised in a catch block that handles the inner exception.

member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: A message that describes the error.

member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: A message that describes the error.
The exception that is the cause of the current exception.

member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is less than min.
member.param: Number to be expected being less than min.
The number that must be less than the argument.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Throw.IfGreaterThan(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is greater than max.
member.param: Number to be expected being greater than max.
The number that must be greater than the argument.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is less or equal than min.
member.param: Number to be expected being less or equal than min.

The name of the parameter being checked.

member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is not in the specified range.
member.param: Number to be expected being greater or equal than max.
The lower bound of the allowed range of argument values.
The upper bound of the allowed range of argument values.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is equal to 0.
member.param: Number to be expected being not equal to zero.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Verify.NotNull(System.Object,System.String)
member.summary: Equivalent of ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.Verify.ValidHostnameSegment(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Validates that a hostname segment string is safe for use as a URL segment, preventing URL injection.
member.param: The hostname segment string to validate (e.g., 'us-east1', 'europe-west4')
Optional parameter name for the exception
member.exception: Thrown when the location contains invalid characters or patterns

member.name: T:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.AppContextSwitchHelper
member.summary: Helper class to get app context switch value

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.AppContextSwitchHelper.GetConfigValue(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns the value of the specified app switch or environment variable if it is set. If the switch or environment variable is not set, return false. The app switch value takes precedence over the environment variable.
member.param: The name of the app switch.
The name of the environment variable.
member.returns: The value of the app switch or environment variable if it is set; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.IListExtensions.AddRange``1(System.Collections.Generic.IList{``0},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})
member.summary: Adds a range of elements from the specified source to the target .
member.typeparam: The type of elements in the list.
member.param: The target to add elements to.
The source containing elements to add to the target .

member.name: T:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.InternalTypeConverter
member.summary: Provides internal utility methods for converting types to strings with consideration for CultureInfo.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.InternalTypeConverter.ConvertToString(System.Object,System.Globalization.CultureInfo)
member.summary: Converts the given object value to a string representation using the appropriate CultureInfo.
member.param: The object to convert.
The CultureInfo to consider during conversion.
member.returns: A string representation of the object value, considering the specified CultureInfo.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.InternalTypeConverter.GetTypeToStringConverterDelegate(System.Type)
member.summary: Retrieves a type-to-string converter delegate for the specified source type.
member.param: The source Type for which to retrieve the type-to-string converter delegate.
member.returns: A Func delegate for converting the source type to a string, considering CultureInfo, or null if no suitable converter is found.

member.name: F:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.InternalTypeConverter.s_converters
member.summary: Converter functions for converting types to strings.

member.name: T:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.NonNullCollection`1
member.summary: Provides a collection of non-null items.

member.name: F:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.NonNullCollection`1._items
member.summary: The underlying list of items.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.NonNullCollection`1.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.NonNullCollection`1.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The initial collection of items to populate this collection.

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.NonNullCollection`1.Item(System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets or sets the item at the specified index in the collection.
member.param: The index of the item to get or set.
member.returns: The item at the specified index.
member.exception: The was not valid for this collection.

member.name: P:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.NonNullCollection`1.Count
member.summary: Gets the number of items in the collection.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.NonNullCollection`1.Add(`0)
member.summary: Adds an item to the collection.
member.param: The item to add.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.NonNullCollection`1.Clear
member.summary: Removes all items from the collection.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.NonNullCollection`1.Contains(`0)
member.summary: Determines whether an item is in the collection.
member.param: The item to locate.
member.returns: True if the item is found in the collection; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.NonNullCollection`1.CopyTo(`0[],System.Int32)
member.summary: Copies all of the items in the collection to an array, starting at the specified destination array index.
member.param: The destination array into which the items should be copied.
The zero-based index into at which copying should begin.
member.exception: The number of items in the collection is greater than the available space from to the end of .

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.NonNullCollection`1.IndexOf(`0)
member.summary: Searches for the specified item and returns the index of the first occurrence.
member.param: The item to locate.
member.returns: The index of the first found occurrence of the specified item; -1 if the item could not be found.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.NonNullCollection`1.Insert(System.Int32,`0)
member.summary: Inserts an item into the collection at the specified index.
member.param: The index at which the item should be inserted.
The item to insert.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.NonNullCollection`1.Remove(`0)
member.summary: Removes the first occurrence of the specified item from the collection.
member.param: The item to remove from the collection.
member.returns: True if the item was successfully removed; false if it wasn't located in the collection.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.NonNullCollection`1.RemoveAt(System.Int32)
member.summary: Removes the item at the specified index from the collection.
member.param: The index of the item to remove.

member.name: T:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.TypeConverterFactory
member.summary: Factory for creating TypeConverter instances based on a provided type.

member.name: M:Microsoft.SemanticKernel.TypeConverterFactory.GetTypeConverter(System.Type)
member.summary: Returns a TypeConverter instance for the specified type.
member.param: The Type of the object to convert.
member.returns: A TypeConverter instance if a suitable converter is found, otherwise null.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.EmptyKeyedServiceProvider
member.summary: Provides an implementation of that contains no services.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.EmptyKeyedServiceProvider.Instance
member.summary: Gets a singleton instance of .

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.RequiredMemberAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that a type has required members or that a member is required.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.FeatureName
member.summary: The name of the compiler feature.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.IsOptional
member.summary: If true, the compiler can choose to allow access to the location where this attribute is applied if it does not understand .

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.RefStructs
member.summary: The used for the ref structs C# feature.

member.name: F:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerFeatureRequiredAttribute.RequiredMembers
member.summary: The used for the required members C# feature.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.IsExternalInit
member.summary: Reserved to be used by the compiler for tracking metadata. This class should not be used by developers in source code.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute
member.summary: Polyfill for the DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute not available in .NET Standard 2.0. Indicates that certain members on a specified are accessed dynamically, for example through .
member.remarks:
This allows tools to understand which members are being accessed during the execution of a program.
This attribute is valid on members whose type is or .
When this attribute is applied to a location of type , the assumption is that the string represents a fully qualified type name.
When this attribute is applied to a class, interface, or struct, the members specified can be accessed dynamically on instances returned from calling on instances of that class, interface, or struct.
If the attribute is applied to a method it's treated as a special case and it implies the attribute should be applied to the "this" parameter of the method. As such the attribute should only be used on instance methods of types assignable to System.Type (or string, but no methods will use it there).

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute.#ctor(System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified member types.
member.param: The types of members dynamically accessed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute.MemberTypes
member.summary: Gets the which specifies the type of members dynamically accessed.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes
member.summary:
Specifies the types of members that are dynamically accessed.
This enumeration has a attribute that allows a bitwise combination of its member values.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.None
member.summary: Specifies no members.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicParameterlessConstructor
member.summary: Specifies the default, parameterless public constructor.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicConstructors
member.summary: Specifies all public constructors.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicConstructors
member.summary: Specifies all non-public constructors.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicMethods
member.summary: Specifies all public methods.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicMethods
member.summary: Specifies all non-public methods.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicFields
member.summary: Specifies all public fields.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicFields
member.summary: Specifies all non-public fields.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicNestedTypes
member.summary: Specifies all public nested types.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicNestedTypes
member.summary: Specifies all non-public nested types.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicProperties
member.summary: Specifies all public properties.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicProperties
member.summary: Specifies all non-public properties.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicEvents
member.summary: Specifies all public events.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.NonPublicEvents
member.summary: Specifies all non-public events.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.Interfaces
member.summary: Specifies all interfaces implemented by the type.

member.name: F:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All
member.summary: Specifies all members.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that an API is experimental and it may change in the future.
member.remarks: This attribute allows call sites to be flagged with a diagnostic that indicates that an experimental feature is used. Authors can use this attribute to ship preview features in their assemblies.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, specifying the ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.
member.param: The ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.DiagnosticId
member.summary: Gets the ID that the compiler will use when reporting a use of the API the attribute applies to.
member.remarks:
The diagnostic ID is shown in build output for warnings and errors.
This property represents the unique ID that can be used to suppress the warnings or errors, if needed.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.ExperimentalAttribute.UrlFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL for corresponding documentation. The API accepts a format string instead of an actual URL, creating a generic URL that includes the diagnostic ID.
member.value: The format string that represents a URL to corresponding documentation.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.AllowNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that null is allowed as an input even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DisallowNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that null is disallowed as an input even if the corresponding type allows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that an output may be null even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that an output will not be null even if the corresponding type allows it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that when a method returns , the parameter may be null even if the corresponding type disallows it.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter may be null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MaybeNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that when a method returns , the parameter will not be null even if the corresponding type allows it.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter will not be null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the output will be non-null if the named parameter is non-null.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the associated parameter name.
member.param: The associated parameter name. The output will be non-null if the argument to the parameter specified is non-null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.NotNullIfNotNullAttribute.ParameterName
member.summary: Gets the associated parameter name.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnAttribute
member.summary: Applied to a method that will never return under any circumstance.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method will not return if the associated Boolean parameter is passed the specified value.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified parameter value.
member.param: The condition parameter value. Code after the method will be considered unreachable by diagnostics if the argument to the associated parameter matches this value.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DoesNotReturnIfAttribute.ParameterValue
member.summary: Gets the condition parameter value.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method or property will ensure that the listed field and property members have not-null values.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with a field or property member.
member.param: The field or property member that is promised to be not-null.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute.#ctor(System.String[])
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the list of field and property members.
member.param: The list of field and property members that are promised to be not-null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullAttribute.Members
member.summary: Gets field or property member names.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the method or property will ensure that the listed field and property members have not-null values when returning with the specified return value condition.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition and a field or property member.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter will not be null.
The field or property member that is promised to be not-null.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.String[])
member.summary: Initializes the attribute with the specified return value condition and list of field and property members.
member.param: The return value condition. If the method returns this value, the associated parameter will not be null.
The list of field and property members that are promised to be not-null.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.ReturnValue
member.summary: Gets the return value condition.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.MemberNotNullWhenAttribute.Members
member.summary: Gets field or property member names.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute
member.summary: Polyfill for the RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute not available in .NET Standard 2.0. Indicates that the specified method requires the ability to generate new code at runtime, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when compiling ahead of time.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of dynamic code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires dynamic code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute
member.summary: Polyfill for the RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute not available in .NET Standard 2.0. Indicates that the specified method requires dynamic access to code that is not referenced statically, for example through .
member.remarks: This allows tools to understand which methods are unsafe to call when removing unreferenced code from an application.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified message.
member.param: A message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.Message
member.summary: Gets a message that contains information about the usage of unreferenced code.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCodeAttribute.Url
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional URL that contains more information about the method, why it requires unreferenced code, and what options a consumer has to deal with it.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute
member.summary: Polyfill for the UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute introduced in .NET 8.0 Suppresses reporting of a specific rule violation, allowing multiple suppressions on a single code artifact.
member.remarks: is different than in that it doesn't have a . So it is always preserved in the compiled assembly.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, specifying the category of the tool and the identifier for an analysis rule.
member.param: The category for the attribute.
The identifier of the analysis rule the attribute applies to.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Category
member.summary: Gets the category identifying the classification of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property describes the tool or tool analysis category for which a message suppression attribute applies.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.CheckId
member.summary: Gets the identifier of the analysis tool rule to be suppressed.
member.remarks: Concatenated together, the and properties form a unique check identifier.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Scope
member.summary: Gets or sets the scope of the code that is relevant for the attribute.
member.remarks: The Scope property is an optional argument that specifies the metadata scope for which the attribute is relevant.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Target
member.summary: Gets or sets a fully qualified path that represents the target of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument identifying the analysis target of the attribute. An example value is "System.IO.Stream.ctor():System.Void". Because it is fully qualified, it can be long, particularly for targets such as parameters. The analysis tool user interface should be capable of automatically formatting the parameter.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.MessageId
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional argument expanding on exclusion criteria.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument that specifies additional exclusion where the literal metadata target is not sufficiently precise. For example, the cannot be applied within a method, and it may be desirable to suppress a violation against a statement in the method that will give a rule violation, but not against all statements in the method.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Justification
member.summary: Gets or sets the justification for suppressing the code analysis message.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.UnreachableException
member.summary: Exception thrown when the program executes an instruction that was thought to be unreachable.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.UnreachableException.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the default error message.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.UnreachableException.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a specified error message.
member.param: The error message that explains the reason for the exception.

member.name: M:System.Diagnostics.UnreachableException.#ctor(System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a specified error message and a reference to the inner exception that is the cause of this exception.
member.param: The error message that explains the reason for the exception.
The exception that is the cause of the current exception.

member.name: T:System.ExceptionExtensions
member.summary: Exception extension methods.

member.name: M:System.ExceptionExtensions.IsCriticalException(System.Exception)
member.summary: Check if an exception is of a type that should not be caught by the kernel.
member.param: Exception.
member.returns: True if is a critical exception and should not be caught.

member.name: M:System.EnvExtensions.GetBoolEnvVar(System.String)
member.summary: Source: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/DiagnosticsOptions.cs Values: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/azure.core.diagnosticsoptions.istelemetryenabled?view=azure-dotnet

member.name: T:ValueTaskExtensions
member.summary: Convenience extensions for ValueTask patterns within .netstandard2.0 projects.

member.name: M:ValueTaskExtensions.AsValueTask``1(``0)
member.summary: Creates a that's completed successfully with the specified result.

member.name: M:ValueTaskExtensions.AsValueTask``1(System.Exception)
member.summary: Creates a that's failed and is associated with an exception.

member.name: M:ValueTaskExtensions.AsValueTask(System.Threading.Tasks.Task)
member.summary: Present a regular task as a ValueTask.
